Ad Text:
About the opportunity

Fyfe is an Australian integrated engineering, environment, planning, and survey firm working at the forefront of a constantly evolving industry. As we continue to grow, were guided by shared values of collaboration, innovation, and meaningful impact.

Heritage is a key part of Fyfe's future. Our Heritage team is growing alongside our infrastructure portfolio and were looking for a Senior Heritage Consultant who wants to be part of that journey, someone who enjoys building, refining, and shaping how heritage services are delivered as the business evolves.

This Sydney based role offers the chance to contribute to a range of active heritage projects across a diverse sector, including large scale renewable energy, the advertised role will primarily support large-scale infrastructure projects across NSW and the Sydney Basin. You'll play a client facing and visible role in strengthening our heritage offering, influencing project delivery approaches, with the opportunity to grow into a leadership role, you will be supporting the development of both people and processes as the team grows.

What you'll be doing

Playing a key role in the delivery and growth of Fyfe's heritage services

Leading high-quality project delivery across a growing portfolio

Taking accountability for project quality in collaboration with the Quality Manager

Leading project planning sessions and helping shape delivery frameworks

Identifying opportunities to improve systems, processes, and efficiencies

Managing project budgets, timelines, and resourcing as the portfolio expands

Supporting and mentoring team members as the Heritage team grows

Building strong, trusted relationships with clients and internal stakeholders

About you

You're a senior heritage professional who enjoys being part of a team in growth mode. You're comfortable working at both strategic and delivery levels and are motivated by the opportunity to help shape how a team operates and evolves.

A degree in archaeology or a related discipline

Excellent communication and relationship-building skills

Experience in client relationship management

A collaborative mindset and experience working in multidisciplinary teams

Leadership capability, with an interest in mentoring and coaching others

A passion for delivering quality innovative solutions

Why Fyfe?

Clear pathways for career progression and leadership development

Flexible working arrangements to suit your lifestyle

A genuinely social, inclusive, and collaborative workplace

Ongoing focus on listening to our people and improving how we work

A full calendar of social and networking events

Purchased leave options via salary sacrifice

Fyfe embraces and fosters a culture that supports diversity, inclusion and respect, where people are empowered to fulfil their potential. We welcome people with diverse skills, experiences, perspectives and backgrounds. We are dedicated and committed to attracting and recruiting Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples and encourage applications from people with disability, people that identify as LGBTQIA+ and people from culturally and linguistically diverse backgrounds.

How to apply

If you believe you meet the above criteria and would like to join our successful finance team, click on the APPLY FOR THIS JOB button to submit your application.

Follow us on LinkedIn to keep up to date with our news and projects.

Please note only shortlisted candidates and those who are Australian Citizens, hold permanent residency will be contacted.

Data Analyst

Overview

We are seeking a hands-on Data Analyst to support a high-priority employment remediation project. This role will focus on integrating, cleansing, and validating complex workforce and payroll data from multiple legacy sources.

Depending on experience, the successful candidate may also take on lead responsibilities, including structuring the dataset and guiding other analysts.

Contract Details

Start: ASAP
Location: Melbourne CBD
Contract Length:4 Months with potential extensions.

Key Responsibilities

Integrate non-standard and fragmented datasets (payroll, HR records, legacy systems, manual inputs) into a single dataset
Clean, structure, and validate data to ensure accuracy and auditability
Identify and resolve data inconsistencies, gaps, and anomalies
Build and maintain Excel-based models and reconciliation frameworks
Support remediation calculations and reporting outputs
Collaborate with HR, Payroll, and Finance teams to validate data integrity
Work at pace to deliver against tight project timelines

For more senior candidates:

Take ownership of the overall data structure and approach
Provide direction and oversight to supporting analysts
Engage stakeholders to ensure alignment on data assumptions and outputs

Skills & Experience

Strong experience working with complex, messy, or incomplete datasets
Advanced Excel capability (data manipulation, formulas, pivots, reconciliation)
Experience in payroll, workforce data, or remediation projects highly desirable
Ability to consolidate multiple data sources into a usable format
High attention to detail with a focus on accuracy and audit requirements
Comfortable working in a fast-paced, delivery-focused environment
Proactive, hands-on mindset with a clear "get it done" approach

All applications should include your resume as a word document.

For more information, visit our website, www.kinexus.com.au or contact James Griffiths on 03 9982 0314.

Workday’s Consulting teams are driven by a passion for our products and the success of our

customers, retain outstanding talent, and deliver satisfied customers

Our Approach to Flexible Work
 

With Flex Work, we’re combining the best of both worlds: in-person time and remote. Our approach enables our teams to deepen connections, maintain a strong community, and do their best work. We know that flexibility can take shape in many ways, so rather than a number of required days in-office each week, we simply spend at least half (50%) of our time each quarter in the office or in the field with our customers, prospects, and partners (depending on role). This means you'll have the freedom to create a flexible schedule that caters to your business, team, and personal needs, while being intentional to make the most of time spent together. Those in our remote "home office" roles also have the opportunity to come together in our offices for important moments that matter.

Help us deliver a better tomorrow
Australia Post is delivering for all Australians. From the vehicles that we drive, to the small businesses that make our communities thrive. We’re delivering for the environment, for our communities, for our customers and for our people. We’re moving forward and we want you to come along for the ride.  




About the opportunity  

As a Data Solutions Lead within the Freight Services team, you will own and drive the definition and delivery of data‑led solutions for priority business use cases. Working closely with business stakeholders and specialist data teams, you will identify where data and analytics can deliver the greatest value, shape clear product requirements, and prioritise delivery to enable data‑driven decision‑making across product strategy, pricing, and freight experience. You will play a key role in modernising the data landscape and establishing foundational enterprise data capabilities aligned to Freight’s long‑term vision.

The role requires strong stakeholder leadership and collaboration, operating as a trusted partner across Freight Services and the broader organisation. You will engage confidently with senior stakeholders, navigate complex delivery environments, and provide clear guidance on trade‑offs, risks, and outcomes. By combining product ownership, technical understanding of data warehousing, and delivery focus, you will help unblock teams and drive sustained progress against Freight Services’ strategic objectives. 

What you’ll deliver with us  

•            Own end‑to‑end delivery of data solutions for defined business use cases, from requirements through to implementation.

•            Prioritise delivery effectively, balancing business value, technical dependencies, delivery risk and platform constraints.

•            Assess use‑case needs and shape delivery approaches, identifying the skills and capabilities required across delivery teams.

•            Influence decision‑making at all levels, helping stakeholders understand insights and the story behind the data to support strategic planning.

•            Support strategic insight delivery and future‑state design, contributing to enterprise data solutions, tooling, strategy, and supporting frameworks.
 

About you  

You will have exceptional business and commercial acumen and a data engineering background with an understanding of data and some knowledge of how that fits in with products or large programs of work.

You’ll also need   

•            Proficient in programming languages and tools used for data engineering and governance such as SQL, Python, CI/CD deployments etc.

•            Strong hands‑on understanding of enterprise data warehouse architectures, including data ingestion, transformation, modelling, and consumption layers.

•            Highly developed understanding of data engineering, data governance, and data modelling best practices, including data quality, lineage, metadata management, and performance optimisation.

•            Experience working with cloud‑based data platforms such as Google Cloud (preferred), AWS, or Azure, including building and operating enterprise data warehouses.

•            Experience supporting analytics, reporting, or self‑service BI platforms built on top of data warehouses.
 

How we’ll deliver for you

Be part of one of the largest programs of work in logistics in Australia.
Develop your experience and thrive in a culture of collaboration.
A great benefits package and market leading culture, hybrid working from our Burnley office with great transport links.

If this sounds like you, please apply today for immediate consideration. 

All CVs will be read by our recruitment team.

Job Description


Drive Strategy. Shape Growth. Enable Transformation.

At Goodman Fielder, we're on a journey to build the future of food — and we're looking for an exceptional Enablement Lead to help shape where we play, how we win, and what comes next.

Sitting at the intersection of strategy, insight, and execution, this role partners directly with senior leaders across the business to drive enterprise‑level thinking, unlock growth opportunities, and translate strategy into real outcomes.

If you're energised by ambiguity, obsessed with insights, and hungry to have meaningful impact, this is a rare and exciting opportunity to influence the direction of one of Australia's most iconic food businesses.

The Opportunity

Reporting to the Head of Growth and Enablement you will:

Lead strategic projects that shape Goodman Fielder's long‑term growth pathway.
Identify new markets, adjacencies, categories, and opportunities using structured analysis and deep insight.
Provide executive leadership with fact‑based recommendations, business cases, and investment options.
Partner cross‑functionally to ensure strategy is understood, embedded, and brought to life.
Turn strategy into clear roadmaps, priorities, and operating rhythms that drive momentum.
Support strategic reviews, transformation initiatives and performance deep‑dives.
Design and deliver enterprise planning cycles and business‑wide cadences.

Your work will directly influence the decisions we make, the investments we prioritise, and the future we build.

Experience We're Looking For

6+ years in strategy, consulting, commercial, insights or portfolio roles (FMCG ideal but not essential).
Experience contributing to or leading enterprise‑level strategic initiatives.
Strong capability in business case development and financial modelling.
Tertiary qualifications in Business, Commerce or related discipline (MBA advantageous).
Proven ability to partner cross‑functionally and influence senior stakeholders.
Customer‑oriented mindset with experience turning customer insight into strategy.

Why Goodman Fielder?

Join a business with iconic brands, a bold growth ambition, and a genuine commitment to transformation. You'll work closely with executive leaders, influence enterprise‑wide choices, and play a central role in shaping the future of our organisation.

If you're looking for a role where your thinking matters and your work drives meaningful change — this is it.

Apply Now!!

Data, Automation, Integration & AI Manager Melbourne | Hybrid 

Let’s be clear, this is no BAU data role!

This is a build, fix, and lead mandate inside a serious business investing heavily in its future.

We’re partnering with a high-performing, privately-owned Australian success story undergoing a major technology and ERP transformation, and they’ve identified one critical gap:

They don’t have a single owner of data truth, integration discipline, automation strategy, and AI enablement.

That’s where you come in.

The Opportunity (Why this role actually matters) Right now:

Data is fragmented
Reporting is inconsistent
Integrations are messy
Automation exists… but not strategically
AI is being explored, but not governed
This hire will make significant changes;
You will:

Establish “Data at the Core”
Define enterprise standards across data, reporting, integration & automation
Turn Power BI into a trusted single source of truth
Move automation from tactical - to scalable services
Introduce AI properly (governed, valuable, controlled)
And most importantly - bring the business on the journey
What You’ll Own

This is a true enterprise-level leadership role without the bureaucracy.
You will:

Set and enforce data, reporting, and integration standards
Eliminate duplicate reporting, conflicting metrics, and poor data quality
Design scalable integration & automation patterns (not band-aids)
Govern change - stop bad decisions before they become technical debt
Build a capability, not just a team
Partner with Architecture, Delivery & the Business to drive real outcomes
You will also have the authority to:

Challenge poor solutions
Pause work that creates long-term risk
Reset direction when needed
(Backed by a CIO who actually wants this done properly)
What Success Looks Like (6–12 months)
A clear, trusted data model across the business
Power BI becomes the front door for decision-making
Manual processes materially reduced through automation
Integration moves from spaghetti - to structured & reusable
AI use cases delivering real business value (not hype)
A visible uplift in capability, standards, and discipline
  
The Environment
350 applications across the landscape
Microsoft-centric stack (Dynamics, Power BI, Azure direction)
Heavy integration challenges across business systems
ERP transformation underway (and needs direction)
Strong appetite for AI, automation, and better decision-making
Translation:

Plenty of complexity.
Plenty of opportunity.
No shortage of problems to solve.
Who This Suits (and who it doesn’t) You are:
A builder of capability, not just a manager of people
Comfortable operating at enterprise architecture + execution level
Someone who can influence senior stakeholders and bring them with you
Able to balance short-term delivery vs long-term strategy
Commercial, pragmatic, and outcome-driven
You are not:
A pure BI/reporting leader
An academic data strategist who can’t execute
Someone who avoids hard conversations
Background We’re Targeting
Enterprise Data / Integration / Automation leadership experience
Experience setting standards across multiple systems & teams
Proven ability to improve:
Data quality
Reporting consistency
Integration maturity
Exposure to AI enablement in a structured, governed way
Experience operating in complex, multi-system environments
Why This Role Stands Out
Backed by a business investing heavily in tech transformation
Real authority to set standards and drive change
Hybrid of strategy + execution (rare to get both)
Entrepreneurial environment - fast decisions, real impact
No red tape, no hiding, no bureaucracy - you own the outcome
The Deal
High base + super + benefits
We want the best
Hybrid working
Long-term transformation runway
Most “Head of Data” roles talk about transformation.
This one actually needs it, so if you’re the type who:

Walks into messy environments
Builds structure, clarity, and trust
And leaves behind something scalable
Then we need to talk, hit APPLY now, and I look forward to meeting you!

About the Role

We are seeking an SAP Capital Planning Specialist to join our expanding Finance & Analytics practice. This role is responsible for designing and delivering enterprise‑grade capital planning, budgeting, forecasting, and investment modelling solutions across SAP platforms.

You will work closely with Finance, Project Management, and Asset‑Intensive business units to support multi‑year capital programs, asset lifecycles, depreciation models, portfolio prioritisation, and scenario planning.

Experience in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC Planning), SAP BPC, SAP Group Reporting, S/4HANA Finance, or SAP Capital Investment Management (IM/PS) is highly regarded.

This role can be based anywhere in Australia or New Zealand, with flexible remote or hybrid options.

Your tasks and responsibilities
Design and deliver SAP capital planning solutions, including investment planning, capital budgeting, multi‑year forecasts, and approvals workflows.
Build and enhance planning models in SAP Analytics Cloud (Planning) or other SAP EPM tools such as BPC or Group Reporting.
Configure capital planning processes including depreciation modelling, asset lifecycle planning, scenario analysis, and cash flow projections.
Work with Finance and Project/Asset teams to translate complex business requirements into scalable SAP planning solutions.
Support reporting and analytics using SAC dashboards, S/4HANA data models, and Group Reporting structures.
Optimise planning processes, improve data quality, streamline workflows, and enhance forecast accuracy.
Document models, assumptions, planning cycles, and user guidance.
Support testing, cutover, deployment, and ongoing Aftercare services.
Your qualifications and experience
Experience in capital planning, financial modelling, enterprise planning, or FP&A.
Practical experience with SAP Analytics Cloud (Planning), SAP BPC, SAP Group Reporting, or similar tools.
Strong understanding of capital project lifecycles, asset accounting, depreciation, and financial planning concepts.
Experience with SAP S/4HANA Finance (FICO), Project Systems (PS), or Investment Management (IM) is highly desirable.
Strong analytical and stakeholder engagement skills, especially with finance and operational teams.
Ability to deliver planning models for large capital portfolios and multi‑year investment cycles.
Excellent communication, documentation, and presentation skills.

Nice to have:

Experience with S/4HANA Public Cloud financial planning features.
Exposure to asset‑heavy industries (energy, utilities, mining, construction, infrastructure).
SAP certifications (SAC Planning, S/4 Finance, BPC, Group Reporting).
What we offer
Work with SAP's latest cloud and planning technologies including S/4HANA, SAC, and BTP.
Flexible and supportive work environment — remote or hybrid.
Competitive remuneration and benefits including salary packaging and development programs.
Access to SAP certifications, structured learning, and continuous enablement.
Be part of a respected SAP Platinum Partner delivering major transformations across ANZ.

Ad Text:
Careers with Impact

More than one million Australians trust us with their money, and that trust shapes how we work every day. Our members sit at the centre of everything we do, guiding our decisions and the impact we strive to make.

We're looking for people who are purpose-driven and accountable, who value inclusion and collaboration, and who are proactive in managing risk to protect our members' long-term outcomes. In return, you'll be part of an organisation where your work contributes to strong returns, real-world impact, and a culture that reflects the communities our members serve.





12 Month Opportunity

Bring your authentic and passionate self to this exceptional role #careerswithimpact

Join HESTA's Office of the CEO team in a strategic, high impact role at the centre of a purpose-led, member first organisation. You'll partner closely with the CEO,Chief of Staff and the Senior Leaders of the organisation to turn strategy into action, sharpen our external voice, and drive initiatives that matter for our members

What you'll do

Shape strategy & planning: Run OCEO planning rhythms (long-term, quarterly, annual) and craft high quality board/executive materials (CEO reports, Board papers and risk and project updates).
Elevate executive presence: Manage the CEO's external engagement pipeline and prepare compelling briefings, speeches, and talking points.
Lead cross functional projects: Drive strategic, external facing initiatives that require strong collaboration and crisp execution.
Deliver insight: Provide strategic and financial analysis; build robust business cases and translate complex issues for senior audiences.
Support special projects (as needed): Contribute to end-to-end M&A activities (target screening, modelling/valuation, due diligence coordination, integration planning).
Lift team performance: Coach team mates, strengthen processes and documentation, and champion a strong risk culture.

What you'll bring

Experience in strategy, planning, and stakeholder engagement (in‑house strategy/Chief of Staff/consulting highly regarded).
Strong financial modelling and analysis skills; proven business case development.
Excellent communication and executive storytelling (PowerPoint) with advanced Excel.
Knowledge of superannuation/financial markets and the regulatory landscape (or the ability to ramp quickly).
Bachelor's in Business, Finance, Economics, or related field; MBA/advanced degree preferred.
You embrace Artificial Intelligence to improve efficiency and uplift quality.

Benefits that matter and make a difference for our employees

Leave for those moments that matter, an additional 6 days of leave at the end of year, up to 6 days paid volunteer leave, gender neutral paid parental leave of 20 weeks, Gender Affirmation leave, reproductive health and wellbeing leave, Cultural and Ceremonial leave. Access your LSL after 3 years, take AL at half pay, and purchase up to 2 weeks additional leave (just to name a few!).
Your Professional development matters, up to $5k per year professional development and up to 8 days professional development leave, HESTA scholarships and free access to a range of premium learning tools.
Your health and wellbeing matters, free annual flu shots and skin checks, incredible social events throughout the year and a comprehensive employee assistance program available 24/7.
Your financial wellbeing matters, up to 15% super, financial planning support, end of year payment for all Enterprise Agreement-covered employees, incentivised Employee Referral Program and novated lease options.

We are seeking an experienced and strategic Senior Director, Coaching Capability for a high-level contract opportunity based in Stirling, ACT, with hybrid working arrangements.

This is a unique opportunity for a senior professional to lead the design, implementation and continuous improvement of a system-wide coaching model within a complex and high-profile environment. The role is focused on strengthening coaching capability across a specialist workforce, ensuring a consistent, high-impact approach that supports quality practice, reflective learning and continuous improvement.

Working in a central leadership capacity, you will be responsible for developing and embedding a coaching framework that supports practitioners to effectively influence professional practice within schools. You will provide structured guidance, modelling, supervision and reflective practice to lift coaching capability and ensure consistency and fidelity across the broader team.

The successful candidate will bring deep expertise in coaching methodologies, adult learning, influence and behaviour change, along with proven experience translating complex theory into practical, repeatable and measurable frameworks. You will be comfortable working across senior stakeholders and specialist teams, with the ability to navigate resistance, build trust and drive capability uplift in complex operational settings.

This opportunity will suit a senior leader with a strong background in coaching capability, professional practice development, organisational uplift, or specialist workforce development. Experience designing sustainable coaching models, embedding reflective practice and supporting multidisciplinary teams will be highly regarded.

Key responsibilities include:

Leading the development, implementation and continuous improvement of a system-wide coaching model
Designing and embedding a consistent, high-impact coaching framework
Building coaching capability through modelling, structured guidance, supervision and reflective practice
Ensuring quality, consistency and fidelity of coaching practice across the team
Translating advanced coaching theory into operational, repeatable and measurable tools and frameworks
Providing expert guidance on influence, resistance, adult learning and behaviour change
Working collaboratively with stakeholders to align coaching practice with broader service priorities.

To be successful, you will demonstrate:

Advanced coaching expertise and experience building coaching capability at scale
Strong experience developing sustainable practice models within specialist or multidisciplinary teams
The ability to operationalise theory into practical frameworks, tools and modules
Excellent stakeholder engagement and influencing capability
Strong written and verbal communication skills
Sound judgement, professionalism and a commitment to high-quality outcomes.

What's on offer:

Initial 5-month contract
Possibility of extension
Hybrid working arrangement
Attractive hourly rate
Opportunity to make genuine system-wide impact in a senior contract role

Join the change. Electrify your future!
We've been proudly Aussie since 1837, always finding new ways to innovate in energy and essential services. Now, we're moving towards a sustainable future through electrification and investing in renewable energy-and we'd like you to join us. Whether you're on-site, in the office, or somewhere in between, you'll find opportunities to grow your career here. You'll work with a team that's passionate about powering Australian lives, in a culture that values inclusivity, respect, and learning. Help us create a cleaner energy future, and we'll back you every step of the way as you build your career.

Join the change. Electrify your future.

About the role
As a Senior Analyst within the Commercial Planning & Insights team, you will play a key role in shaping sales and pricing strategy across AGL's residential and SME segments. Leveraging data and analytics, you'll generate insights that enhance commercial performance, optimise customer experience, and support market growth.

You'll analyse customer behaviour, market trends, and competitor activity to inform strategic decisions, while monitoring key sales metrics and daily performance. Working with complex datasets, you'll translate insights into actionable recommendations, helping to drive competitive, data-led commercial outcomes.

What you'll be doing:

Drive Commercial Insights: Generate hypotheses and deliver data-driven insights on sales, pricing, and overall commercial performance to inform strategic decisions.
Advanced Data Analysis & Storytelling: Analyse complex datasets and translate findings into clear, compelling insights through visualisation and storytelling.
Performance Monitoring & Reporting: Track key metrics through weekly reporting and executive-level updates, identifying trends in sales, churn, and growth opportunities.
Market & Customer Intelligence: Analyse market dynamics, competitor activity, and customer segments (residential & SME) to uncover opportunities and shape strategy.
Stakeholder Engagement & Impact: Partner cross-functionally with sales, marketing, product, and pricing teams to influence outcomes and continuously improve data quality and performance measurement.


About you:

Previous demonstrated experience as an Analyst or working within a customer insights or consulting role, preferably within the energy or utilities sectors.
Strong analytical skills with experience in data analysis and visualisation tools (i.e. SQL, PowerBI).
Experience in executive-level reporting writing and analysis.
Ability to develop and maintain dashboards, reports and key performance indicators (KPIs).
Excellent communications skills with the ability to convey insights and recommendations to sales teams and stakeholders.

At AGL, we offer a wide range of benefits including:

Flexible working options including hybrid work.
Discounts on energy, telcos and solar plans.
Participate in Energise - our reward & recognition program.
Access 20 weeks of paid parental leave for the primary carer.
Access to an additional week of recharge leave per year.
Play an active role in our diversity & inclusion initiatives.
Novated leasing and Electric Vehicle subscriptions.
Access to study assistance, AGL's share purchase plan and insurance deals.

Job Title: General Manager, Enterprise Strategy and Business Transformation
Ad Text:
The organisation

Aspect (Autism Spectrum Australia) is Australia’s largest autism-specific service provider, supporting people on the autism spectrum and their families from early childhood through adulthood. With specialist schools, community programs, therapy services, and national partnerships, Aspect’s purpose, a different brilliant®, is about understanding, engaging and celebrating the strengths, interests, and aspirations of people on the autism spectrum and creating inclusive environments where everyone can thrive.

The opportunity

Reporting to the Chief Executive Officer and leading Enterprise Strategy and Business Transformation, the General Manager will:

Lead the development and delivery of Aspect’s enterprise strategy and strategic planning cycle.
Drive organisation-wide transformation aligned to the 2026–2030 Strategic Plan.
Provide strategic advice to the CEO and Executive Team on priorities, opportunities and risks.
Lead digital, data and technology functions to enable innovation, improved service delivery and scalability.
Oversee impact measurement, research and reporting to strengthen evidence-based decision making and sector leadership.
Establish and lead government relations and advocacy to influence policy and system reform.
Embed strong program, project and change management disciplines across the organisation.
Drive performance insights, benefits realisation and data-led decision making.
Build strategic partnerships across government, research and community sectors.
 
About you

Key skills, essential criteria and desirables:

Significant senior executive experience leading strategy and transformation in a complex organisation.
Proven experience delivering large-scale enterprise transformation and strategic planning.
Strong digital, data and systems thinking capability.
Demonstrated ability to lead multidisciplinary teams and drive organisational change.
Exceptional stakeholder engagement and influencing skills across government and sector partners.
Relevant tertiary qualifications in business, commerce or a related discipline
 
What’s on Offer

Key executive leadership role reporting to the CEO.
Opportunity to shape a newly established Strategy and Transformation function.
Lead enterprise-wide transformation and innovation.
Influence sector leadership, policy and long-term organisational sustainability.
Work within a values driven organisation supporting people on the autism spectrum.
National scope with significant strategic impact.

Enterprise Data Standards Specialist (12-Month Contract)

Sydney, Brisbane, Hybrid | Lead enterprise data standards, governance and integrity




Introduction

Our client is seeking a highly experienced Enterprise Data Standards Specialist to play a key role in establishing and uplifting data governance, standards and integrity across a complex global organisation.

This role will provide leadership and oversight for the design and implementation of enterprise-wide data standards, frameworks and control mechanisms that ensure data is consistent, trusted and aligned to business objectives. The focus will be on strengthening data quality, governance maturity and accountability across multiple domains including Sales, Account Management, Customer, Operations and Finance, enabling high-quality insights, forecasting and commercial decision-making.

Working closely with executive stakeholders and cross-functional teams, you will guide the organisation in embedding best-practice data governance, driving behavioural change and promoting a culture of data stewardship. You will play a critical role in translating complex data dependencies into clear business outcomes, ensuring that data standards directly support customer insights, operational efficiency and commercial value creation.

This is a 12 month fixed-term engagement (with potential extension), supporting a significant enterprise-wide data uplift initiative designed to enhance data integrity, governance maturity and the organisation’s ability to leverage data as a strategic asset.


About 9X5 Consulting

We are transforming the way business and technology is managed by putting real-time data into the hands of every decision maker across organisations. The insight garnered from diverse backgrounds, perspectives and lived experiences results in pioneering innovations across the organisation and better experiences for our customers. The more diverse our talent, the more impact we have on each other and on our valued clients.

Our clients range from small SME's right through to some of the largest ASX listed organisations in Australia, so experience at selling to stakeholders at this level is required.


A bit about you

You are a senior data governance leader with a strong track record of designing and embedding enterprise data standards across complex, global organisations. You understand that high-quality, trusted data is the foundation of commercial insight, operational efficiency and customer value.

You bring a strategic mindset combined with the ability to translate complex data frameworks into practical, business-aligned outcomes. You are comfortable operating at an executive level, influencing stakeholders across Sales, Operations, Finance and Technology to drive alignment and accountability.

Exceptional communication skills are mandatory in this role. You will be required to clearly articulate complex data concepts, dependencies and risks into meaningful business impacts, ensuring engagement and adoption across diverse global teams.


Key Responsibilities                           

As the Enterprise Data Standards Specialist, you will provide strategic leadership and governance oversight across the organisation’s data landscape, ensuring enterprise data standards are designed, implemented and sustained in alignment with business and commercial objectives. You will drive the uplift of data integrity, governance maturity and accountability, enabling trusted data to underpin decision-making, customer insights and operational performance.

Your responsibilities will include:

Establish and govern enterprise-wide data standards, policies and control frameworks

Provide strategic leadership to uplift data governance maturity and capability

Drive alignment between data standards and commercial, operational and customer outcomes

Communicate data policies and insights across global and regional stakeholders

Deliver executive-level reporting on data quality, integrity and risk

Champion data stewardship and behavioural change across the organisation

Enable data-driven decision-making, forecasting and customer insights

Support platform convergence and future innovation through robust data frameworks




Key Deliverables

Cultural Adoption – Establish clear, enforceable ownership of data across global and regional operations, shifting the organisation from reactive data correction to proactive data stewardship.

Data Literacy Program – Design and implement a structured data literacy initiative that builds organisational capability, drives accountability and embeds consistent understanding of data standards across all job families.

Data Standards Framework – Develop and implement the formal structures, definitions, business rules, controls and artifacts required to ensure data is globally consistent, trusted, reusable and delivers measurable commercial value.

Data Governance Model – Establish the governance structure, authority, accountability and decision-making mechanisms required to ensure the Data Standards Framework is consistently applied, enforced and sustained across all regions and business units.

Business Process Re-Engineering (BPR) – Lead the redesign of end-to-end business processes to simplify operations, eliminate inefficiencies, strengthen data controls and improve performance, customer outcomes and overall business value.




Job Requirements

This role requires a highly experienced data governance leader who has successfully delivered enterprise-wide data standards and control frameworks within large, complex organisations.

You will be comfortable operating across global business functions, partnering with executive stakeholders and driving alignment between data governance and commercial outcomes. You will have a proven ability to influence behaviour, embed accountability and uplift data maturity across diverse teams.

Essential

10+ years’ experience in data governance, including 5+ years in senior leadership roles

Proven experience designing and implementing enterprise data standards and control frameworks

Strong stakeholder engagement skills with experience influencing executive leadership

Experience aligning data governance with commercial, operational and customer objectives

Ability to resolve complex data ownership and data quality challenges across global environments

Tertiary qualification in Information Management, Business Information Systems, Data Governance, Analytics or similar

Desirable

Experience in high-volume, global industries (travel, logistics, retail or similar)

Certifications such as CDMP, ISO 8000, ITIL 4 or COBIT

Personal attributes

To be successful in this role you will demonstrate:

Strategic thinker with strong commercial acumen

Highly influential with the ability to drive behavioural change

Strong communicator who can simplify complex concepts

Results-driven with a focus on measurable outcomes

Collaborative leader who builds trust across global teams

High attention to detail with a strong governance mindset

Deal-breakers (stuff we don’t want in a new team member)

A “that’ll do” approach to our clients’ projects;

A closed mind to change;

Big egos. We’re really, really not keen on big egos here.




This is a senior leadership role requiring both strategic oversight and hands-on delivery of enterprise data governance initiatives. Please submit your application via SEEK to be considered for this opportunity.


*Only applicants with citizenship, permanent visa will be considered.

As the Interaction, Performance & Analytics Manager, you will lead a specialist analytics and insights team that provides critical operational and interaction intelligence across Sydney Water’s Customer Experience Group. Your work will help the business understand how customer interactions perform, where improvements are needed, and how data can be used to drive better customer outcomes and operational efficiency.

The opportunity

The Privacy Specialist plays a key role in shaping and strengthening QBE’s approach to privacy and responsible data use. Working as part of the Group Privacy Team, this role partners closely with the business to manage privacy risk, uplift capability, and embed privacy‑by‑design across data, technology and AI initiatives.

About QBE  

At QBE, we get to the heart of what matters for our customers. And we do it all with a human touch.  

We’re an international insurer with more than 13,000 people working across 26 countries – which means we’re big enough for your ambitions, yet small enough for you to make a real impact. It’s an exciting time. We’re building momentum towards our vision to become the most consistent and innovative risk partner.

What if you could have a positive impact – at work and in the world? As part of the QBE team, you’ll get to spend every day working with people who are passionate, talented and kind.

Your new role

As Privacy Specialist, you will support QBE’s Group and AUSPAC Division by providing trusted, pragmatic privacy advice while helping to evolve our privacy strategy, governance and risk management maturity. You’ll work collaboratively across the organisation, influencing outcomes and ensuring privacy considerations are embedded into business decision‑making.

Key responsibilities include:

Delivering high‑quality privacy advice, including undertaking Privacy Impact Assessments (PIAs) and managing privacy incidents, complaints and enquiries

Partnering with Technology, Data, AI and project teams to identify, assess and manage privacy, ethical and regulatory risks

Embedding privacy‑by‑design, ethical design and responsible AI principles across initiatives and programs

Supporting the implementation of Group privacy, data and AI policies within the AUSPAC business

Contributing to the continuous improvement of privacy policies, standards, training and awareness across the organisation

Supporting privacy governance, reporting, metrics and insights for senior leaders and key stakeholders

About you

You are a confident privacy professional with deep subject matter expertise and a pragmatic, solutions‑focused approach. You enjoy partnering with the business, can balance competing priorities, and are comfortable operating autonomously in a complex, fast‑paced environment. You bring strong judgement, clear communication skills and a collaborative mindset.

Key requirements:

Proven experience advising on privacy risk, Privacy Impact Assessments and incident management

Strong knowledge of privacy obligations, data ethics and emerging technologies, including AI

Experience working in large, complex organisations with diverse stakeholder groups

Demonstrated ability to prioritise, triage and manage multiple privacy matters effectively

Strong stakeholder engagement, influencing and communication skills

Experience in insurance or financial services is advantageous, but not essential

Benefits  

We offer a range of benefits to help provide holistic support for your work life, whatever your circumstances. As a QBE employee you will have access to and benefit from:

‘Hybrid Working’ – a mix of working from home and in the office to enhance your work/life balance

Free holistic wellbeing coaching, nutritional, confidential counselling, financial and legal advice

18 weeks’ gender-equal flexible leave for all new parents, including paid super

Ad Text:
At Australian Retirement Trust we’re building resilient portfolios that deliver strong, long term outcomes for our members. We’re looking for an experienced Senior Portfolio Manager – Planning to be our strategic lead for multi-year business planning across the investment business area, turning strategy into an aligned plan, clear priorities and measurable delivery.

Why join us?

You'll get 14% super as standard, to help your future come alive.
Unleash your potential and build a career to be proud of through excellent training and development opportunities.
Balance your work between in-person days at our purpose-built hubs and working from home.
In the office, enjoy end of trip facilities, a yoga and multi-faith room, parent and carer rooms, quiet rooms, BrewHub coffee facilities, and collaborative team spaces.

About the role

We’re all about helping our members make the most of their money. And while they go after their goals, you can too. This is a highly influential role at the centre of how the Investment business area sets direction and delivers.  You’ll lead the multi‑year business planning cycle, ensuring the investment business area strategic priorities translate into a coherent roadmap with clear priorities and measurable outcomes.

Reporting to the General Manager, Sustainable Investment & Planning, you’ll work closely with senior leaders across Investments and partner teams (including risk, finance, investment operations and investment legal) to align assumptions, trade‑offs and delivery commitments and provide high‑quality reporting for executive and committee forums.

Day to day, you'll:

Lead the multi‑year planning process across the Investments business area, ensuring alignment with ART’s enterprise strategy and long-term investment outcomes.
Shape strategic options and recommendations for senior leaders including prioritisation, sequencing, interdependencies and key trade‑offs.
Partner with internal stakeholders to align inputs and drive end‑to‑end development and execution of multi‑year business plans.
Oversee the delivery of strategic initiatives, including defining success measures and tracking outcomes.
Provide high‑quality insights, reports and recommendations to senior leadership and the Investment Committee on progress, outcomes, risks and opportunities.
Monitor industry, regulatory and market developments to inform strategic priorities.
Build and maintain strong relationships with internal and external stakeholders.
Represent ART on committees and at industry forums, contributing thought leadership and enhancing our brand.
Lead, develop and support a high‑performing team in an inclusive and values‑driven environment.

This role is a permanent opportunity. 

📍 Location: Sydney or Brisbane.

About you

You're a strategic thinker who can connect the dots across a complex function turning ambition into aligned, executable multi-year roadmap. You are a natural collaborator with experience in investments and the ability to influence senior stakeholders through clear, evidence-based recommendations. You’ll likely tick some of these boxes:

You hold relevant tertiary qualifications, with a degree or postgraduate qualification in finance, investments, economics, commerce, engineering or a related discipline.
You have deep experience in the investments industry, with strong knowledge of legislation, regulation, market dynamics, risk return optimisation and emerging industry trends.
You bring exceptional research and analytical capability, using data, insights and evidence to inform strategic, innovative thinking.
You’re highly skilled in stakeholder management and negotiation, and you’re comfortable influencing senior leaders and executive audiences.
You’ve successfully led and developed teams, and you’ll foster an inclusive, high performance culture that supports growth and delivery.

About us

Our 2.4 million members trust us to take care of over $330 billion in retirement savings. And we help them take charge of their finances and face the future with confidence. Our inclusive culture means you’ll be valued and heard in a respectful workplace. We've created an environment where you can be yourself and do amazing work. It's that simple.

Apply online now

Everyone is welcome to apply. We value diverse thinking, cultures, perspectives, backgrounds, and abilities. Awaken your career at Australian Retirement Trust and apply now. Applications close Sunday, 12 April 2026. 

We don’t want to leave you with a monstrous wait. So, you’ll hear from us after the closing date to find out whether you’ve made it through to the next round or not. Being fair and up-front is important to us – and recruitment is no different.

The Opportunity

A client in high-growth mode is seeking an experienced data and insights professional to lead data, reporting and systems capability within a large-scale, nationally significant operational environment.

This is a senior BAU leadership role with a strong uplift mandate—ideal for someone who thrives on bringing structure, clarity and performance to complex, fast-growing organisations.

You will play a pivotal role in enabling data-driven decision making, improving system and data platform effectiveness, and ensuring high-quality reporting across a substantial and expanding portfolio.

You will also lead a team responsible for data governance, reporting and systems, ensuring these functions support delivery, performance and executive decision-making.

Key responsibilities include:

Owning enterprise data, reporting frameworks and core operational systems
Delivering high-quality daily, weekly and monthly operational reporting
Translating complex operational activity into clear, decision-ready insights
Improving system effectiveness (including CRM platforms) and reducing manual workarounds
Partnering with technology teams to define and deliver system enhancements
Establishing strong data governance, integrity and assurance frameworks
Leading external and executive reporting requirements
Building and leading a high-performing team

Current State

You will step into a function that is operationally critical but requires uplift, including:

Fragmented data across multiple platforms
Low adoption of core systems
Manual reporting processes and workarounds
Rapidly growing portfolio with significant delivery pressure

This role will be instrumental in defining problems, driving alignment, and implementing practical solutions.

About You

You are a senior data and insights professional who sits at the intersection of data, reporting, systems, and risk, and who combines strategic thinking with hands-on delivery.

Proven leadership experience in data, reporting or operational intelligence functions
Strong capability in data governance, reporting frameworks and performance insights
Experience in enhacing or implementing business-critical technology systems

Reporting to the Head of Risk & Compliance, this position is accountable for managing operational risks arising from operations within the Enterprise Services division. Develop and deliver the Line 1 assurance plan, ensuring alignment with higher-risk areas and regulatory obligations.

Establish and monitor Key Risk Indicators (KRIs); analyse trends and escalate breaches promptly.

Lead proactive assessments to identify process improvement opportunities, including streamlining, automation, and the design or enhancement of controls to strengthen resilience and efficiency.

Strong financial and economic modelling capability, with the ability to translate complex analysis into clear, actionable recommendations.

Confident operating in ambiguity and applying commercial judgement to challenge assumptions and improve decision quality.

The Role

We are on the lookout for a Senior Manager – Data/AI Governance to lead the development and rollout of enterprise governance practices, with data governance at the core.

This role is fundamentally focused on establishing strong data governance foundations—ensuring data is accurate, secure, well-managed, and compliant. Building on this, you will help shape how AI is governed across the organisation, recognising that effective AI governance depends on high-quality, well-governed data.

You’ll operate in a hands-on capacity initially, with the opportunity to build out a team over time. Working across business, technology, and risk, you’ll embed governance into day-to-day operations and enable the organisation to scale its data and AI capabilities safely.

Main Responsibilities
Design and implement data governance frameworks, policies, and standards as the foundation for broader AI governance
Embed governance across the full data lifecycle

Partner with business units and data owners to strengthen accountability and ownership
Support AI and model governance, ensuring alignment with core data governance principles

Contribute to governance forums and senior stakeholder reporting
Support vendor and delivery governance standards
Key Skills & Experience
Strong experience in data governance as a core discipline

Ad Text:
Drive meaningful transformation by delivering expert business analysis, process improvement and project support across BAI’s enterprise portfolio.




WHY BAI COMMUNICATIONS

It’s an exciting time to be part of BAI Communications. Building on our achievements in broadcasting, telecommunications and critical communications, we continue to expand our operations, helping connect people and support communities.

 

We operate one of the world’s most extensive broadcast networks, delivering fully managed television and radio services to 99% of the Australian population. During crises such as natural disasters, national broadcasters depend on BAI to maintain vital communications, while emergency services trust us to keep them connected and informed.




As 5G technology transforms connectivity, BAI is leveraging global experience to enable comprehensive coverage in challenging environments—buildings, tunnels, stadiums, and high-density areas. This expertise positions us at the forefront of intelligent, connected infrastructure development across Australia.




Our acquisition of Titan ICT has strengthened our mission-critical capabilities, adding deep expertise in mining, resources, and energy industries. This expansion enables us to deploy robust solutions across Australia’s most demanding operational environments.




Discover more through BAI360, our virtual tour platform. 

 

LET’S EXPLORE THE ROLE

As a Business Analyst – Transformation, you will play a key role in delivering BAI’s enterprise transformation agenda. Working across multiple initiatives, you will partner with business leaders, project teams and technical specialists to analyse complex requirements, simplify processes and ensure transformation initiatives deliver measurable value.




This is a hands-on, adaptable role suited to someone who thrives in a fast-paced environment, can balance multiple priorities, and brings strong relationship‑building skills. In addition to core business analysis, you’ll provide flexible support across test management and change management activities as required to ensure successful project delivery.

 

Key Responsibilities:

Lead a variety of business analysis activities across transformation initiatives
Elicit, document and manage high-quality business, functional and non-functional requirements
Map current and future state processes, identifying opportunities for simplification, automation and efficiency
Facilitate workshops, stakeholder interviews and requirements sessions to drive shared understanding and buy-in
Manage requirements throughout the project lifecycle, ensuring alignment with business objectives and delivery constraints
Provide flexible project support, contributing to test management and change management activities where needed
Champion continuous improvement across business processes and transformation outcomes
Produce clear, compliant documentation including process maps, user stories, test artefacts and project deliverables
Provide regular insights and reporting on progress, risks, dependencies and outcomes




LET’S HEAR ABOUT YOU

You are an experienced Business Analyst with a strong background in transformation and complex project environments. You combine analytical rigour with strong people skills and are confident working across diverse stakeholder groups.

You bring expert capability in business analysis and process improvement, along with advanced facilitation, documentation and stakeholder engagement skills. You’re adaptable, solutions-focused and comfortable contributing beyond traditional BA boundaries to support successful delivery.

 

Technical Skills Required:

Strong command of business analysis and end-to-end requirements management
Highly developed capability in process mapping, analysis and continuous improvement
Demonstrated strength in stakeholder engagement, workshop design and facilitation
Proven proficiency using Microsoft Visio, Excel and the Microsoft Office Suite
Well-established experience eliciting requirements, developing user stories and defining acceptance criteria
Hands-on experience with PowerBI and data visualisation techniques
Practical experience working across Agile and hybrid delivery methodologies
Sound experience supporting test management, UAT coordination and change management activities
Solid working knowledge of enterprise architecture principles, ITIL frameworks and system implementation concepts




BENEFITS & CULTURE

About the Catholic Development Fund

Catholic Development Fund (CDF) are a values-based financial institution that provides funding and support across its footprint of Catholic organisations, including parishes, primary and secondary schools, healthcare, aged care, and social services providers.

Our work enables real impact: from hospital wings for the sick, safe havens for the homeless, and classrooms for the young – we support our customers to grow their mission. Through our expertise and commitment, we help Catholic organisations put their faith into action and contribute to a fairer, more cohesive society.

About the Role

We're seeking a creative and motivated Process Improvement Specialist, Retail to join our Process Transformation team. This is a pivotal role focused on transforming how our clients interact with our Retail Fund, CDF Community Fund – from their first enquiry through to ongoing engagement and retention.

The Process Improvement Specialist will work closely with the Client Experience Specialist and is responsible for implementing initiatives and championing continuous improvement to enhance the end-to-end Community Fund experience. The role focuses on ownership of forms and processes, automation, data quality uplift, regulatory compliance, CRM readiness, and system optimisation to support organisational transformation and ensure a seamless, scalable client journey.

Key Accountabilities

Community Fund Process Optimisation

Support and implement the full applicant-to-onboarding workflow to reduce manual effort, improve transparency, and enhance client experience.
Implement automation opportunities (forms, workflows, notifications, approvals) across the Community Fund lifecycle.
Map and document current-state and future-state procedures and processes.
Partner with Marketing, Client Services, Data, and IT teams to implement process enhancements.

CRM Migration Support – Client Data and Process Readiness

Assess, cleanse, and structure Community Fund client data to ensure readiness for Dynamics 365 CRM migration.
Develop data quality rules, validation logic, and remediation steps for legacy datasets.
Partner with Data and IT and CRM squads to embed process logic, workflow requirements, and business rules into the CRM design.

KYC Compliance and Risk Management

Support the Executive Manager Retail in a KYC uplift program for the Community Fund, addressing non-compliance with existing client records.
Design improved KYC collection, verification, and evidence workflows aligned to regulatory obligations.
Implement sustainable mechanisms to maintain ongoing compliance through digital capture and automated reminders.

Core Banking Platform – Functionality Optimisation

Analyse current use of core banking platform functionality as it relates to the Community Fund.
Identify opportunities to streamline tasks, reduce manual workarounds, and leverage underused features.
Work with vendors and internal product teams to test and implement improvements.

Reporting, Insights & Performance Measurement

Track key process performance metrics including processing times, data quality, KYC completion, and automation effectiveness.
Provide regular insights and recommendations to the Executive Manager, Process Transformation.
Support continuous improvement cycles with data-driven analysis.

Cross-Functional Collaboration

Partner with Marketing and Community Engagement teams on client-focused initiatives.
Collaborate with Relationship Managers to understand client needs and enhance service delivery.
Work with Risk and Compliance teams to ensure client processes meet regulatory requirements.

What You Bring

Essential

Demonstrated experience in process improvement, business analysis, or transformation roles.
Strong capability in process mapping, optimisation, and automation design.
Experience working with CRM systems (Dynamics 365 preferred) and data cleansing/migration activities.
Understanding of regulatory compliance frameworks including KYC/AML.
Ability to engage diverse stakeholders and simplify complex concepts.
Strong digital literacy and experience implementing digital-first solutions.
Outstanding written and verbal communication skills.
Proven ability to manage multiple projects and stakeholders simultaneously.
Collaborative mindset with strong relationship-building skills.

Highly Desirable

Experience in financial services, banking or regulated industries.
Exposure to core banking systems.
Lean Six Sigma or continuous improvement certification.
Background in digital transformation or process improvement initiatives.
Experience with CRM systems (e.g., Microsoft Dynamics 365).

Why work for us?

Generous salary package - that recognises your skills, experience, and contributions.
Professional Environment - join a dedicated team of professionals committed to fostering an agile and inspiring work environment.
Meaningful Work - be part of a values, driven organisation that makes a difference in the community.
Hybrid Flexibility - we offer a hybrid working model (3 days in the office per week), plus a modern workspace in tranquil East Melbourne.

How to apply

If you're looking for a role where you can make a real impact, we want to hear from you!

To submit an application, please click apply and provide a copy of your current resume and a cover letter.

Please note: We will be reviewing applications on a rolling basis, if you believe this role might be for you, please apply as soon as possible.

Employment is subject to satisfactory background checks which include a National Police Check, Working with Children Check and Reference Checks. To be successful in this role you must hold valid working rights in Australia.

At Acenda Group, everything we do is driven by a clear purpose: to help our customers and their families live, save and retire with confidence.

We bring together some of Australia and New Zealand’s most trusted life insurance brands. With over 176 years of combined heritage and deep expertise across our local markets, our Group includes Acenda Life, Resolution Life Australasia, and Asteron Life New Zealand.

Backed by the global strength of Nippon Life, we are one of the largest life insurance businesses across Australia and New Zealand. More than two million customers rely on us for life insurance, wealth protection and retirement solutions that support their confidence at every stage of life.

We’re building an inclusive, dynamic and future focused business. Joining us means stepping into a period of integration and growth, where your work will help shape our future.

If you’re energised by change and motivated by purpose, this is your moment to make an impact.

The role

Reporting into the Head of Digital, the Digital Enablement and Experience Lead is a newly created role within the function.

You will be responsible for uplifting Acenda’s enterprise digital capability across platforms, portals, digital experiences and end‑to‑end customer, adviser and partner journeys.

As we transition from a fragmented multi‑portal landscape to a unified, scalable and efficient digital environment, you will continue to drive improvements to the customer journey experience across our Group, Retail and Retirement lines.

You will be responsible for safeguarding the continuity and simplification of our digital assets, enhancing user experiences, and enabling sustainable growth across all channels and propositions.

About you

An experienced Digital Manager, you possess a minimum of 7+ years of broad digital experiences across MarTech, digital enablement and experience design within the financial services sector.

You have a proven track record in enabling and delivering tangible customer journey improvements across enterprise digital platforms, apps, and portals in regulated environments.

You ideally have been involved in projects or possess prior experience in consolidating digital estates, overseeing platform migrations and standardisation whilst maintaining and optimising operational capabilities.

Lastly, you possess strong influencing and leadership skills with the ability to lead multi-disciplinary digital teams in a hybrid and agile delivery environment.

Join us in building confidence— for our customers, our communities and each other.

·       Your work makes a real impact. Everything we do helps our customers and their families live, save and retire with confidence, and your contribution will directly support that purpose.

·       You’ll help drive meaningful change. As an inclusive, dynamic and adaptable organisation, we offer a unique opportunity for you to grow while shaping the future of our organisation.

·       You’ll feel supported and included. Our team culture is flexible, collaborative and people‑centred, with hybrid working to help you balance life and work.

·       By making things simpler. We help our people operate efficiently, adapt easily and spend more time on meaningful work that drives personal and organisational growth.

·       We celebrate success. Our incentive and recognition programs ensure great work is acknowledged and rewarded.

·       Your wellbeing comes first. We offer tools, resources and benefits that support your whole self, including additional lifestyle leave.

We celebrate the diversity of our customers and community, and we embed diversity, equity, inclusion, and belonging into how we do business. We encourage applications from people of all backgrounds, cultures, experiences, and identities.

Should an applicant be the preferred candidate, background checks (including Federal Police Checks, Employment checks, ASIC banned and disqualified persons and Bankruptcy checks) will be completed prior to the candidate's employment being confirmed. The outcomes of the background checks do not automatically bar candidates, however they will be assessed against the inherent requirements of the position.

The role

As Insurance Analyst, you will partner with Corporate and Asset-based stakeholders to provide operational and administrative support to the Insurance function on all insurance-related matters.

You will work closely with insurance management, broker and the business to provide insurance support and optimise performance of the insurance function.

You will work with the insurance management to ensure GPT's insurance program is effectively managed, risks are appropriately mitigated, and compliance standards are maintained across all assets and entities, while implementing efficiencies to streamline responsibilities and deliverables.

Working across diverse internal teams and with external brokers and insurers, you will play a key role in protecting and managing risk across the business.

What you will be doing

Provide day-to-day operational and administrative support to the Insurance function, including responding to insurance-related queries and providing guidance across all of GPT's insurances.
Support the review of supplier and tenant insurance requirements to ensure compliance with contractual requirements and GPT standards.
Assist with preparation of insurance renewal documents including insurer questionnaires, presentations and prospectus documents.

Job Title: Senior Data Engineer / Senior Analytics Engineer
Ad Text:
Senior Data Engineer

At Elula, we partner with some of Australia's top financial institutions to tackle a wide range of business challenges through cutting-edge AI-as-a-service (AIaaS). Our vision? To make the complex simple and easy, delivering amazing customer experiences and personalisation that matters.

Our products leverage machine learning, productionised models, data pipelines, explainable AI, and rich visualisations to drive customer engagement, retention and insights. We're constantly pushing the envelope, uncovering the ever-evolving needs of customers and staying ahead of the curve.

Join a team of some of the brightest minds in data science and engineering; experts with years of experience and a passion for delivering smarter decision-making. It's a fast-paced, high-performing and collaborative environment where creativity and learning take centre stage, and the work is as fun as it is rewarding.

Find out more about us at https://elula.ai/team/.




The role

At Elula, data is the foundation of our AI-as-a-Service platform. As a Senior Data Engineer, you'll be the person who truly understands that data: where it comes from, what it means, how it behaves, and how to shape it into something reliable and useful.

Most of your best work will happen before you write any code; in understanding what the data represents, where it came from, and how it fits into our products. You'll spend your time interrogating complex datasets, designing thoughtful data models, and building the transformation logic that turns raw inputs into trusted, high-quality outputs powering our models and products. You won't just move data - you'll make sense of it.

You'll work autonomously on open-ended problems alongside data scientists, software engineers, and customer-facing teams, contributing across the full data lifecycle from ingestion and modelling through to validation and delivery. We're a collaborative, ambitious team, and we're looking for someone who takes genuine ownership, communicates clearly across disciplines, and isn't afraid to ask the hard questions about the data in front of them.

To be successful in this role, you will be expected to demonstrate the following skills and experience.
 

What You’ll Bring

Data transformation and modelling

A deep curiosity for data - you naturally interrogate datasets to understand their shape, quality, and fitness for purpose before building anything.

Strong applied data modelling skills, designing structures that serve analytical, operational and downstream use cases well.

The ability to develop and optimise complex SQL across large datasets, with a sharp eye for correctness, performance and long-term maintainability.

Comfortable using Python to automate workflows and building reusable tooling that makes the team more effective.

Ownership and judgement

You own your work end-to-end, from design decisions through to ongoing reliability, exercising sound technical judgement when trade-offs arise and proactively keeping stakeholders informed.

You manage your own priorities and delivery with discipline, and help your team do the same without waiting to be asked.

When something's wrong with the data, you dig in, diagnose it systematically, and fix it pragmatically, then share what you learned so the same issue doesn't come back.

 Technical leadership

You communicate complex data concepts clearly to both technical colleagues and non-technical stakeholders - translating between the two comes naturally.

You actively support and uplift junior engineers, sharing context, approaches and best practices generously.

You bring curiosity and a continuous improvement mindset, staying across new tools and techniques and contributing to raising the bar for the whole team.

You know how to get the most out of AI-assisted development tools like Claude Code - using them as an amplifier to think bigger and spend your energy on problems that genuinely require human judgment.




Nice to Have

While the following are not required, they are highly valued.

A background in a quantitative discipline such as computer science, mathematics or statistics.

Experience working in the financial services industry (e.g. banking, insurance or superannuation)


Benefits

Elula’s people are at the core of all we do. Our benefits are just one way we show that.

Enjoy flexible working hours, and work from home two days per week, to better balance your personal and professional life.

Experience the freedom of working anywhere in Australia up to five days annually.

Give back to what matters to you with extra paid annual leave for volunteering.

Put your health first with an allowance for fitness and wellbeing.

Drive your development with financial support for approved training courses.

Level up with matched funding for career coaching.

Job Title: Head of FP&A - Tier 1 Private Equity backed
Ad Text:
Allura Partners are delighted to be partnering exclusively with a PE backed business in Sydney on a search for a Head of FP&A. Reporting to the CFO, the Head of FP&A will partner with finance, operations and leadership to build a scalable finance function. You will lead budgeting, forecasting, reporting & analysis, while supporting M&A integration and driving commercial insights across the business.

Duties/Key responsibilities

Build and establish the FP&A function from the ground up, designing scalable processes, frameworks and reporting standards to support a high-growth, acquisition-led business
Own and lead the full planning cycle including annual budgets, rolling forecasts, and long-range plans, delivering high-quality, decision-ready insights to senior leadership
Deliver best-in-class monthly and quarterly reporting, including detailed variance analysis, performance narratives and proactive identification of risks and opportunities
Act as a strategic business partner to operational leadership, driving accountability through KPI frameworks and embedding a performance-led culture across the organisation
Play a key role in M&A execution by leading financial integration, standardising processes and building robust frameworks to track and realise synergies across acquisitions
Strengthen financial governance, controls and data integrity across systems and reporting outputs
Develop and maintain robust financial models to support scenario analysis, investment decisions, and strategic initiatives
Skills and experience

Degree in finance, accounting or related field (CA or CPA preferred)
12+ years’ experience in FP&A or broader finance roles
Background in private equity or transaction advisory advantageous
Strong financial modelling and advanced Excel skills
Experience with BI/ERP systems (e.g. Power BI, Tableau, NetSuite)
Ability to communicate financial insights to non-financial stakeholders
Strong commercial mindset and analytical capability
If your experience matches the brief, please apply using the link. Thank you
Calum Forster
